seo.title: Dreamina Seedance 2.0 Mini | Low-Cost AI Video, Specs & Pricing | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use Dreamina Seedance 2.0 Mini on MaxVideoAI for lower-cost 480p/720p AI video batches, ecommerce variants, UGC hooks, video editing, extension, and reference-guided tests.

overview: Dreamina Seedance 2.0 Mini is the cost-performance Seedance AI video route on MaxVideoAI for lower-cost batch/value work: ecommerce variants, marketing tests, UGC hooks, multimodal references, video editing, and extension in 4-15 second 480p or 720p clips.
pricingNotes: Dreamina Seedance 2.0 Mini uses token-based generation pricing. MaxVideoAI applies the display price and shows the final quote before submission.

hero.title: Dreamina Seedance 2.0 Mini — lower-cost route for batch video variants
hero.intro: Use Dreamina Seedance 2.0 Mini when you need a value route for many 480p/720p video variants before committing hero shots to Seedance 2.0. This page tracks specs, prompts, pricing, and scorecards for batch-focused generation.

promptStructure.title: Prompting Seedance 2.0 Mini
promptStructure.quote: [Batch goal] + [subject or product] + [one action beat] + [reference job] + [video editing or extension need] + [480p/720p] + [4-15s].
promptStructure.description: Keep each Mini prompt focused on one variant so batch comparisons stay readable.
steps: Describe the product, creator, or scene
Assign each multimodal reference one job
Move winning shots to Seedance 2.0 when final polish matters

tips.title: Production notes
items: Use 480p for volume checks and 720p when reviewers need more detail.
Keep batches organized by one product, hook, or edit goal.
Use multimodal references to guide identity, product shape, camera rhythm, or audio mood.
Use video editing and extension for lower-cost source-video iteration before a final route.
Keep Mini positioned for batch/value exploration before Seedance 2.0 final/polished work.
Generate shows the active route and final quote before you submit.

prompts.title: Ecommerce product batch
prompts.prompt: Create an 8 second 720p 16:9 product motion variant for a matte black speaker on a kitchen counter, slow push-in, clean morning light, use the product image reference for shape accuracy.

prompts.title: UGC hook set
prompts.prompt: Generate a 4 second 480p vertical UGC hook: creator lifts the skincare bottle toward camera, one bright bathroom beat, natural handheld motion, batch version A for opening-frame comparison.

prompts.title: Video editing pass
prompts.prompt: Use the source video as a lower-cost video editing pass, keep the subject and product framing stable, adjust pacing for a marketing cut, 8 seconds at 720p.

prompts.title: Extension test
prompts.prompt: Extend the short source clip into a 15 second 720p product ending with a smooth camera drift and clean final pose for review.

faqs.q: What is Seedance 2.0 Mini best for?
faqs.a: Seedance 2.0 Mini is best for lower-cost batch/value work such as ecommerce variants, marketing tests, UGC hooks, video editing, extension, and reference-guided 480p/720p motion checks.

faqs.q: What resolution and duration does Seedance 2.0 Mini support?
faqs.a: The MaxVideoAI Mini route exposes 480p or 720p output, 4-15 second durations, and 24fps.

faqs.q: Does Seedance 2.0 Mini support audio and lip-sync?
faqs.a: Yes. Mini supports native synchronized audio and lip-sync, and audio references can guide rhythm or mood.

faqs.q: When should I use Seedance 2.0 instead of Mini?
faqs.a: Use Seedance 2.0 when you need the final, polished, higher-ceiling Seedance route with higher-resolution delivery and stronger production quality. Use Mini for batch/value exploration before that step.

decisionCards.title: Mini or Seedance 2.0?
decisionCards.body: Use Mini for lower-cost batch/value exploration. Move approved hero shots to Seedance 2.0 when the final needs more polish, higher-resolution delivery and the higher Seedance ceiling.

decisionCards.title: Mini or Fast?
decisionCards.body: Use Fast when speed is the draft priority. Use Mini when repeatable cost-performance across many variants matters more.

decisionCards.title: Need prompt examples?
decisionCards.body: Start with batch hooks, ecommerce product loops, reference-guided video editing and extension prompts.

section.title: Prompt Lab — Seedance 2.0 Mini
section.intro: Seedance 2.0 Mini works best with prompts where each run answers one batch question: product angle, hook, reference behavior, video editing pass, or extension.
section.referencesTitle: How Dreamina Seedance 2.0 Mini uses references

tabs.label: Text
tabs.title: Text batch prompt
tabs.description: Use this when you want a lower-cost read on framing, motion, hook, or audio direction from language alone.
tabs.copy:
Batch question:
[What this run should prove: hook, camera, product angle, motion, or sound]

Subject and action:
[One readable subject + one visible action]

Camera:
[One move only: push-in / tracking / static / handheld]

Style:
[Lighting, weather, palette, realism level]

Audio setting:
[Audio on for native ambience/dialogue, or audio off for visual-only review]

Rule:
Do not test more than one major variable in the same Mini run.

tabs.label: Refs
tabs.title: Image and audio reference prompt
tabs.description: Use this when Mini should keep a product, person, wardrobe, or mood stable across many variants.
tabs.copy:
Prompt intent:
[What the generated clip should do]

Image refs:
@Image1 = [product / person / composition]
@Image2 = [style / wardrobe / palette]

Audio refs if attached:
@Audio1 = [rhythm / ambience / dialogue texture]

Motion:
[One clear action or camera move]

Continuity anchors:
[What must not drift across variants]

Rule:
Each reference gets one job. Do not upload competing references for the same role.

tabs.label: Edit/Extend
tabs.title: Video editing or extension prompt
tabs.description: Use this when a source clip already exists and Mini is testing a cheaper edit or continuation.
tabs.copy:
Source clip:
[What already happens and what must stay continuous]

Requested change:
[Edit the look / adjust pacing / extend the ending]

Do not change:
[Subject identity, product shape, location, camera logic]

New beat:
[One action, reveal, or final pose]

Audio:
[Keep off for visual review, or enable native audio for ambience/SFX/dialogue tests]

Rule:
Describe the next change, not an unrelated new scene.

tabs.label: Compare
tabs.title: Batch comparison prompt
tabs.description: Use this when Mini is producing several comparable options before a final Seedance 2.0 pass.
tabs.copy:
Fixed setup across all variants:
[Subject, ratio, resolution, duration, references, and core action]

Variant A:
[Only one changed variable]

Variant B:
[One different variable]

Success criteria:
[What wins: clarity, product read, emotional hook, motion, or cost]

Handoff note:
[What should move to Seedance 2.0 if approved]

Rule:
Keep duration and resolution fixed so cost and quality comparisons stay clean.

tabNotes.body: Text = one batch question from language only.
Refs = keep product, subject, or style stable.
Edit/Extend = use source clips for lower-cost revisions.
Compare = hold settings fixed and change one variable.

globalPrinciples: Keep one product, hook, or edit goal per run.
Use 480p for volume and 720p for review detail.
Assign each image, video, or audio reference one job.
Use source video inputs for video editing and extension tests.
Move the strongest result to Seedance 2.0 when final polish matters.
engineWhy: Text prompts are best for batch ideation and low-cost marketing hooks.
Image references keep product shape, wardrobe, or packaging consistent.
Video references and source videos help test pacing, edits, and extensions.
Audio references can guide rhythm or mood, while native audio/lip-sync can be generated with the clip.

demo.title: Demo prompt — Seedance 2.0 Mini
demo.prompt:
8 second 720p 16:9 cinematic golden-hour city crosswalk scene. A father and his small daughter move through the crowd as a bus passes behind them and the street suddenly fills with upward motion.
Shot 1: medium-wide street-level view, normal city life around them, documentary push forward, warm rim light and long shadows.
Shot 2: smooth slow-motion push-in as the background reacts naturally; keep the father and child as the emotional focus, hands connected and faces realistic.
Shot 3: close medium on the child looking up with quiet awe, then the father smiles with relief. Warm sunlight flickers across the final frame.
Audio setting for this curated render: off. For new Mini renders, enable native audio if you want traffic ambience, footsteps, pass-by sound, or short dialogue. Constraints: no readable text, no logos, no subtitles, no UI.

summary.subject: Father and child in a golden-hour city crosswalk
summary.action: A wave of motion crosses the scene as a bus passes behind them
summary.camera: Documentary push-in, then an emotional close medium shot
summary.style: Warm light between buildings, moving silhouettes, cinematic realism
summary.output: Selected render is audio off; enable native audio for new sound tests
